message:
test(concurrency): finish migrating remaining bare lock-timeout literals to shared constant

Follow-up to the initial CONTENTION_SUCCESS_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS migration:
three more call sites in `tests/e2e_concurrency.rs` still had bare
`"25"` / `"50"` lock-timeout literals that were missed on the first
pass — both inside the sync-status observer loop and in two branches
of the external-access-and-background-status-bounded-during-mutation
test.

Replace all three with `CONTENTION_SUCCESS_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS` (the
existing `"500"` constant) so the whole file now shares one source of
truth for "how long to wait before a concurrent call may give up and
report contention." No other behavior change; the literal values the
tests were previously using (25 / 50 ms) were below the realistic
contention window on a loaded CI box and occasionally starved out the
success branch of the "at least one must succeed" assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
